title: Circuit Complexity & Proof Complexity

boolean_circuits.why: Boolean circuits = algebraic gates over F_2: EML-0 (no transcendentals)

circuit_size.size: S(f) = number of gates: algebraic count = EML-0
circuit_size.depth: D(f) = longest path: algebraic count = EML-0

monotone_circuits.why: Approximation method: exp(-ε·s) probability bounds = EML-2

razborov_approximation.why: Probabilistic approximation argument uses real exp = EML-2

clique_lower_bound.result: CLIQUE requires exp(√n) monotone circuit size

proof_size.formula: S(π) = number of lines in proof: EML-0 (count)

cutting_planes.formula: CP proof size ~ exp(n): EML-2 lower bound

switching_lemma.formula: Håstad switching lemma: Pr[decision tree depth>k] ≤ (5p)^k

parity_lower_bound.formula: PARITY requires exp(n^{1/(d-1)}) AC⁰ size (depth d)

algebraic.why: VP/VNP = polynomial families over field: EML-0 (algebraic, no transcendentals)

new_finding.insight: VP vs VNP is purely EML-0: algebraic geometry over finite fields

semiring_verdicts.new_finding: Lower bound methods (EML-2) act on EML-0 objects: depth gap reveals proof vs object

key_theorem: Circuit-Proof Semiring Theorem (S286): Circuit/proof objects are EML-0; lower bound methods are EML-2. Boolean gates (AND/OR/NOT): EML-0 — pure algebraic, no transcendentals. VP/VNP algebraic complexity: EML-0 — geometric over fields. Lower bounds (Razborov approximation, Håstad switching lemma, PHP lower bounds): EML-2 — all use real exponential probability bounds. NEW FINDING: depth stratum reveals the OBJECT-METHOD GAP: circuit OBJECTS are EML-0 but LOWER BOUND PROOFS are EML-2. This gap (EML-0 objects proved by EML-2 methods) may be why P≠NP is hard: natural proofs barrier = EML-0 circuits resist EML-2 approximation arguments.
rabbit_hole_log: Boolean circuits: EML-0 (algebraic gates over F_2)
Lower bounds: EML-2 (probabilistic approximation = real exp)
NEW: object-method gap: EML-0 objects proved by EML-2 methods
P≠NP hardness may reflect: EML-0 ↔ EML-2 cross-stratum resistance
